text.title: Diary Study Examples
text.summary: Two worked examples showing study design, entry prompts, findings, and insights for a 14-day onboarding study and a 30-day cross-device study.

example.note: 14-day interval-contingent study of new app users. Research question: how does understanding evolve from day 1 to day 14?
example.code:
Daily entry prompt (event-contingent): - What did you do with the app today? - Anything confusing? - Anything you discovered? - How confident do you feel using it? (1-5)
Findings by phase: - Days 1-3: Focus on core features, confusion about navigation structure. - Days 4-7: Discovery of secondary features, "aha moments" with advanced functions. - Days 8-14: Established usage patterns emerge; some features never accessed.
Key insight: Users form stable habits by day 7. Features unused by day 14 are almost never subsequently adopted. Implication: onboarding must surface key features within the first 7 days.

example.note: 30-day event-contingent study of cross-device behavior. Research question: when and why do users switch devices?
example.code:
Entry prompt (triggered each device switch): - What device did you use? - Why this device and not another? - What were you trying to do? - Screenshot if possible.
Findings by time-of-day: - Morning: phone (quick checks, 1-3 minutes per session). - Work hours: desktop (complex tasks, sustained sessions). - Evening: tablet (browsing, reading, media consumption).
Switching triggers: - Task complexity drives desktop shift (forms, multi-step processes). - Location drives mobile shift (commute, waiting, movement). - Content type drives tablet shift (video, long reads).
Implication: design the mobile experience for task completion under 60 seconds; desktop for multi-step workflows.

rule.statement: Use rolling synthesis, not batch analysis. Run a theme-synthesis pass weekly from day 3 so emerging patterns can generate follow-up questions while participants are still active.
rule.rationale: Late synthesis produces a complete retrospective analysis of an unprobed dataset. Themes identified by day 7 become the raw material for exit interview questions on day 14.

example.note: Per-entry classification prompt pattern for agent coding.
example.code: Entry: {text} Attached media: {image_caption_if_any} Classify: usable | vague | off-topic. If usable, extract: trigger, context (location/time/mood), task, outcome (success/failure/partial), notable_quote. Return JSON.

example.note: Weekly synthesis prompt for identifying theme evolution.
example.code: Entries from week {n}, segment {seg}: {entries_json} Identify 3-5 emergent themes. For each: theme, count of entries supporting, representative quote with participant_id, change vs week {n-1}. Flag themes weakening (strong last week, absent this week).
